evals.prompt: I want coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) to work well in this repo. Set up agent instruction docs for it — whatever files make sense.
evals.expected_output: A lean AGENTS.md (commands verified against package.json incl. pnpm, required env vars, the load-bearing legacy/ dir) plus Claude Code compat via CLAUDE.md symlink or @AGENTS.md import. No directory-tree dumps or discoverable stack prose.
assertions: Creates AGENTS.md as the canonical instruction file at the repo root
Provides Claude Code compatibility: a CLAUDE.md symlink to AGENTS.md or a CLAUDE.md containing an @AGENTS.md import
Every command listed exists in package.json scripts (no invented commands) and respects the pnpm packageManager field
Documents required env vars (DATABASE_URL, STRIPE_API_KEY) or points to .env.example as required-at-boot
Captures the non-discoverable gotcha: src/legacy/ looks dead but is load-bearing (quarter-end CSV imports) and must not be deleted
Stays lean: no directory-tree dump, no stack overview prose restating package.json, file under ~80 lines

evals.prompt: Our CLAUDE.md has grown into a mess over the past year and honestly the agent seems to ignore half of it now. Can you review it and clean it up?
evals.expected_output: Identifies and fixes the seeded problems: stale commands, dead paths, the async contradiction, prettier-enforced rules, vague lines, emphasis inflation, directory dump. Result is a much shorter accurate file with justified removals.
assertions: Removes or flags the stale commands: 'npm run e2e' (not in package.json) and './scripts/deploy.sh' (file does not exist)
Fixes dead path references: src/db/ (code moved to src/data/) and docs/API.md (does not exist)
Resolves the contradiction between 'always use async/await' and 'prefer .then() chains' (keeps at most one or flags it)
Removes style rules already enforced by .prettierrc (semicolons, single quotes, 100-char lines)
Removes vague/aspirational lines ('write clean code', 'be careful', 'follow best practices', 'never break the build') and the directory-tree dump
Final file is at most half the original's line count while retaining the real commands (start, test, lint, build, format)
Removals are justified to the user (a summary or per-removal reasons, with uncertain items flagged as questions rather than silently deleted)

evals.prompt: We just finished migrating our tests from Jest to Vitest, and 1.8 added a required REDIS_URL env var (service exits at boot without it). Update our agent docs to match.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md updated: vitest commands incl. single-file invocation, jest mock convention replaced, REDIS_URL requirement noted. The drifted .cursorrules is also fixed or consolidated. File stays lean.
assertions: AGENTS.md test commands updated to Vitest, including a working single-file invocation (e.g. npx vitest run src/foo.test.ts)
The Jest mock convention line is replaced or removed; no remaining Jest references in AGENTS.md
REDIS_URL is documented as required (service exits at boot without it)
The drifted .cursorrules (still says Jest) is addressed: updated, consolidated into AGENTS.md, or its removal proposed
File stays lean: no unrelated new sections, no directory maps, final AGENTS.md within ~10 lines of the original length
